heroCompanyDescription: West African Resources Limited (ASX: WAF) is an ASX-listed gold producer with its primary operating asset, the Sanbrado Gold Mine, located in Burkina Faso, West Africa. The company poured first gold at Sanbrado in 2020 and has rapidly grown into a significant mid-tier producer, reporting a Net Profit After Tax of A$567 million for the 2025 financial year -- a result that underscores the mine's exceptional cash generation capacity at elevated gold prices.

skew.rationale: The probability skew is modestly bullish, driven by the extraordinary gold price environment (US$5,024/oz) and confirmed A$567M NPAT that together suggest the current A$2.94 price embeds an unusually large risk premium. The combined N1 and N4 upside scenarios carry 42% probability versus 18% for the bear case, with the base case (N2) at 40% reflecting genuine uncertainty around jurisdictional risk and capital allocation. The key risk to this skew is a gold price reversal or Burkina Faso security deterioration.

verdict.text: At A$2.94, WAF is priced as a high-risk, single-jurisdiction gold producer with a meaningful probability of operational disruption -- a characterisation that appears increasingly difficult to reconcile with a A$567M NPAT delivered in FY2025 and gold trading at US$5,024/oz. The market is applying a structural Burkina Faso discount that, while historically justified, may be overstated at current gold prices given the company's demonstrated operational resilience. The 25% gap between the current price and the 52-week high of A$3.91 suggests the stock has already absorbed significant negative sentiment, creating an asymmetric setup where the upside scenarios (N1 + N4 = 42%) modestly outweigh the downside (N3 = 18%). The key risk to this view is that the jurisdictional discount is rational and persistent, and that gold prices mean-revert before Kiaka can be de-risked. We initiate with a modestly bullish skew, pending a full valuation build and clarity on the Kiaka development timeline.

featuredRationale: The hypothesis weightings assign 40% to the base case (steady-state producer with jurisdictional discount), 35% to the bull case (gold-driven re-rating), 18% to the bear case (jurisdictional deterioration), and 7% to the tail catalyst (Kiaka-driven transformation or M&A). The combined upside scenarios (42%) modestly outweigh the downside (18%), producing a net bullish skew, though the large base case weight reflects genuine uncertainty about the pace and magnitude of any re-rating.

identity.overview: West African Resources Limited (ASX: WAF) — auto-added to coverage. Full company overview pending analyst research.

hypotheses.title: N1: Growth/Recovery
hypotheses.description: With gold trading at US$5,024/oz and WAF delivering A$567M NPAT for 2025, the market may be materially undervaluing the company's earnings power at current gold prices. A sustained gold price above US$4,500/oz combined with operational consistency at Sanbrado and the Kiaka development project could drive a significant re-rating toward the 52-week high and beyond. This scenario requires continued gold price strength, disciplined capital allocation, and successful Kiaka project execution.
supporting: Gold at US$5,024/oz (+2.9% on the day) represents an extraordinary revenue tailwind for a low-cost West African producer
A$567M NPAT for FY2025 confirms the company is generating substantial free cash flow at current gold prices
Current price of A$2.94 sits 25% below the 52-week high of A$3.91, suggesting meaningful upside if earnings momentum is sustained
Investor Presentation released March 2026 signals management confidence in communicating the growth story to the market
contradicting: Gold prices are inherently volatile; a reversal from US$5,000/oz toward US$3,500/oz would materially compress earnings
West African jurisdictional risk (Burkina Faso) remains a structural discount factor that limits re-rating potential
No broker consensus data available to confirm whether the A$567M NPAT beat expectations or was already priced in

hypotheses.title: N2: Base Case
hypotheses.description: WAF continues to operate Sanbrado as a profitable, cash-generative gold mine in Burkina Faso, delivering strong NPAT but trading at a persistent discount to Australian-domiciled peers due to West African political and security risk. The A$2.94 price reflects a market that acknowledges the earnings quality but applies a meaningful risk premium to the jurisdiction. This base case assumes gold prices remain elevated but the jurisdictional discount does not compress materially.
supporting: A$567M NPAT for FY2025 confirms operational execution and cost discipline at Sanbrado
Corporate Governance Statement filed March 2026 indicates adherence to ASX governance standards, supporting institutional investor confidence
Current price of A$2.94 is mid-range within the 52-week band of A$2.08-A$3.91, consistent with a market in a holding pattern
Cessation of securities notification (March 12, 2026) suggests options/performance rights expiry, reducing near-term dilution overhang
contradicting: Gold at US$5,024/oz should arguably be driving a stronger re-rating if the base case is truly priced in
Absence of dividend announcement despite A$567M NPAT may signal capital is being directed to Kiaka development, creating uncertainty about capital returns

hypotheses.title: N3: Risk/Downside
hypotheses.description: Burkina Faso has experienced significant political instability, military coups, and security deterioration in recent years, and a further escalation could threaten WAF's ability to operate Sanbrado or advance Kiaka. Forced renegotiation of mining agreements, operational disruptions from security incidents, or outright suspension of operations would destroy the earnings thesis regardless of the gold price. This scenario would likely see the stock test or break below the 52-week low of A$2.08.
supporting: Burkina Faso has experienced two military coups since 2022 and ongoing jihadist insurgency in the Sahel region
West African gold miners trade at a structural discount to peers in safer jurisdictions, reflecting this embedded risk
No specific operational risk disclosures or security updates were available in the evidence set to assess current ground conditions
Absence of media/news coverage in the evidence set makes it difficult to rule out emerging operational issues
contradicting: A$567M NPAT for FY2025 confirms operations were uninterrupted through the full year
Management released an Investor Presentation in March 2026, signalling confidence in ongoing operations
Gold at US$5,024/oz provides a substantial financial buffer to absorb cost increases from security measures

hypotheses.title: N4: Disruption/Catalyst
hypotheses.description: WAF's Kiaka gold project in Burkina Faso represents a potential step-change in production scale that could transform the company from a single-asset mid-tier producer into a multi-asset major. A positive Kiaka construction decision, combined with gold above US$4,500/oz, could attract M&A interest from larger gold majors seeking West African exposure or drive a re-rating to a materially higher multiple. This tail scenario requires both gold price support and successful de-risking of the Kiaka development timeline and capital cost.
supporting: Strong balance sheet implied by A$567M NPAT provides internal funding capacity for Kiaka development
Gold at US$5,024/oz dramatically improves Kiaka project economics and NPV
West African gold assets have historically attracted M&A interest from majors (e.g., B2Gold, Endeavour Mining) at significant premiums
contradicting: Kiaka development in Burkina Faso carries the same jurisdictional risk as Sanbrado, potentially limiting major acquirer interest
No specific Kiaka development update or timeline was included in the March 2026 announcements reviewed
Capital intensity of a new mine build in a challenging jurisdiction could strain the balance sheet if gold prices retreat

narrative.theNarrative: [Coverage Initiated 17-Mar-26 20:19 AEST] The dominant narrative for West African Resources (ASX: WAF) at initiation is one of exceptional earnings power meeting persistent jurisdictional discount. The company has just reported a A$567 million NPAT for FY2025, a result that would be celebrated as transformational for any mid-tier gold producer -- yet the stock sits at A$2.94, some 25% below its 52-week high of A$3.91. The backdrop could hardly be more supportive: gold is trading at US$5,024/oz, up 2.9% on the day of initiation, representing a generational high for the commodity. The central tension is straightforward -- WAF is generating extraordinary cash flows from Sanbrado, but the market is applying a structural discount to reflect the reality that the mine sits in Burkina Faso, a country that has experienced two military coups since 2022 and ongoing Sahel security deterioration. The Investor Presentation released on March 17, 2026 signals management is actively working to close this valuation gap by communicating the earnings story to investors. The key near-term question is whether the Kiaka development project will receive a construction decision, which would be the single most powerful re-rating catalyst available to the company. Until that decision is made, and until the market gains greater confidence in Burkina Faso's operational stability, WAF is likely to trade at a discount to its intrinsic value -- but that discount, at current gold prices, appears unusually wide.

priceImplication.label: Embedded Assumptions at A$2.94
priceImplication.content:
At A$2.94, the current price appears to embed the following assumptions:

1. Jurisdictional Risk Premium: A 30-40% discount to Australian-domiciled peers on equivalent earnings, reflecting Burkina Faso political and security risk.

2. Gold Price Mean Reversion: The market likely assumes gold reverts toward US$3,500-4,000/oz over the medium term, compressing future earnings from the FY2025 peak of A$567M NPAT.

3. Kiaka Optionality Discounted: Limited value is being ascribed to the Kiaka development project, either due to capital cost uncertainty, jurisdictional risk, or timeline uncertainty.

4. No Near-Term Capital Returns: The absence of a dividend announcement despite A$567M NPAT suggests the market expects capital to be directed toward Kiaka development rather than returned to shareholders.

5. Single-Asset Concentration Risk: The price reflects the vulnerability of a company whose entire earnings base depends on one operating mine in a challenging jurisdiction.

narrative.evidenceCheck: The evidence base for this initiation is materially constrained across most domains. The two strongest data points are the verified A$567M NPAT for FY2025 (from the market-sensitive ASX announcement of March 16, 2026) and the gold price of US$5,024/oz (measured macro data), which together anchor the earnings and commodity context. Corporate communications are available but carry the inherent promotional bias of investor presentations. Critically, no broker research, consensus estimates, production guidance, AISC data, or detailed balance sheet metrics were available in the evidence set, making it impossible to assess whether the NPAT beat or missed expectations, what the cost structure looks like, or how leveraged the balance sheet is. The absence of media coverage, competitor disclosures, and alternative operational data creates significant blind spots around jurisdictional risk, relative efficiency, and market sentiment. The governance filing (Appendix 4G) provides minimal analytical value beyond confirming ASX compliance. Overall, the evidence base supports a high-level directional view but is insufficient for precise valuation work.
narrative.narrativeStability: The current narrative -- exceptional earnings power discounted by jurisdictional risk -- is moderately stable in the near term but faces several potential destabilisers. The most acute risk is a deterioration in Burkina Faso security conditions that forces operational curtailment at Sanbrado; this would immediately invalidate the earnings thesis regardless of the gold price. Conversely, the narrative could shift sharply bullish if WAF announces a Kiaka construction decision or if a major gold producer makes a takeover approach, both of which would force the market to re-price the jurisdictional discount. The gold price itself is the most powerful swing factor -- a sustained move above US$5,000/oz would make the current discount increasingly difficult to justify, while a reversal below US$3,500/oz would validate the market's caution. The next formal test of the narrative is the Q1 2026 quarterly production report, which will confirm whether Sanbrado is operating at full capacity and provide updated cost guidance.

evidence.intro: Evidence is assessed across ten epistemic domains, ranked by reliability. Each domain carries a distinct epistemic quality — from verified regulatory filings to unverified media commentary — ensuring the analyst understands what the evidence is before assessing what it means.

cards.title: 1. Corporate Communications
cards.finding: West African Resources Limited's Investor Presentation (March 2026) and the 'WAF Delivers $567 Million NPAT for 2025' announcement highlight robust financial performance. The company reported a Net Profit After Tax (NPAT) of $567 million for 2025, indicating strong profitability and operational success.
cards.tension: Investor presentations are inherently promotional and may selectively present data to portray a positive outlook, potentially downplaying risks or future challenges not explicitly mentioned.
cards.source: Investor Presentation March 2026 (2026-03-17), WAF Delivers $567 Million NPAT for 2025 (2026-03-16)

cards.title: 2. Regulatory Filings & Financial Statements
cards.finding: West African Resources Limited reported a Net Profit After Tax (NPAT) of $567 million for the 2025 financial year, as detailed in their market-sensitive announcement and 2025 Annual Report on March 16, 2026. This strong financial result confirms significant profitability and operational success for the period.
cards.tension: While the NPAT is strong, a full review of the annual report is needed to assess cash flow generation, debt levels, and specific operational costs that could impact future financial stability.
cards.source: WAF Delivers $567 Million NPAT for 2025 (2026-03-16), 2025 Annual Report (2026-03-16)

cards.title: 3. Broker Research
cards.finding: No specific broker research or consensus estimates are provided in the input data. However, the reported NPAT of $567 million for 2025 is a significant figure that would likely lead to positive revisions or confirmation of strong performance in any existing analyst models.
cards.tension: Without actual broker reports, it is impossible to ascertain if the reported NPAT met, exceeded, or fell short of consensus expectations, or what future price targets and recommendations are.
cards.source: Data Limitation (No specific broker research provided)

cards.title: 4. Competitor Disclosures
cards.finding: No specific competitor disclosures or peer company announcements are provided in the input data. WAF operates in the West African gold mining sector, which includes several other listed producers.
cards.tension: Without direct competitor data, it is difficult to assess WAF's relative operational efficiency, cost structure, or market share within the broader West African gold mining landscape.
cards.source: Data Limitation (No specific competitor disclosures provided)

cards.title: 5. Economic Data
cards.finding: The price of gold (GC=F) is currently US$5024.3, showing a significant increase of +2.9% today. This high and rising gold price provides a strong tailwind for gold miners like WAF, directly boosting potential revenue and profitability.
cards.tension: While the current gold price is highly favourable, commodity prices are inherently volatile and subject to global economic shifts, geopolitical events, and central bank policies, posing a risk to sustained high prices.

cards.title: 6. Alternative Data
cards.finding: No specific alternative data such as operational indicators (e.g., production volumes, AISC), ESG reports, or channel checks are provided in the input. The strong reported NPAT suggests efficient operations, but granular data is absent.
cards.tension: The lack of specific operational metrics or ESG performance data makes it difficult to independently verify the efficiency of WAF's mining operations or assess potential non-financial risks and opportunities.
cards.source: Data Limitation (No specific alternative data provided)

cards.title: 7. Academic Research
cards.finding: No specific academic research or industry studies on the West African gold mining sector or structural trends are provided in the input data. This limits the ability to contextualize WAF's operations within broader industry dynamics.
cards.tension: Without academic research, it is challenging to understand long-term structural trends, technological advancements, or specific geopolitical and environmental risks pertinent to the region and WAF's operations.
cards.source: Data Limitation (No specific academic research provided)

cards.title: 8. Media & Social
cards.finding: No recent news headlines, earnings/results news, or social media commentary regarding WAF are provided in the input data. This limits insight into public sentiment or broader market narratives.
cards.tension: The absence of recent media coverage makes it difficult to gauge public perception, market commentary, or any emerging narratives (positive or negative) that might not be captured in official company announcements.
cards.source: Data Limitation (No recent news headlines or social media provided)

cards.title: 9. Leadership & Governance
cards.finding: WAF released an Appendix 4G & Corporate Governance Statement on March 16, 2026, indicating adherence to ASX corporate governance principles. This suggests a commitment to transparency and sound management practices, which is a positive for investor confidence.
cards.tension: While a governance statement is provided, specific details on board independence, executive remuneration, or recent insider trading activities (beyond cessation of securities) are not available to fully assess leadership quality or potential conflicts of interest.

cards.title: 10. Ownership & Capital Flows
cards.finding: A 'Notification of cessation of securities - WAF' was announced on March 12, 2026, which typically relates to the expiry or forfeiture of unlisted options/performance rights, potentially reducing future dilution. The current share price of A$2.94 is within its 52-week range of A$2.08 - A$3.91.
cards.tension: While cessation of securities can be positive, the specific reasons and volume are not detailed, making it hard to assess its full impact. No information on institutional holders, fund flows, or the broader capital structure (e.g., debt-to-equity) is provided.
cards.source: Notification of cessation of securities - WAF (2026-03-12), Current Price: A$2.94, 52-Week Range: A$2.08 - A$3.91

discriminators.intro: The following data points carry high diagnosticity — they can meaningfully shift probability between competing hypotheses.

rows.evidence: Sanbrado Q1 2026 gold production volume and AISC (All-In Sustaining Cost per ounce) -- the single most diagnostic operational metric for distinguishing between N1 (strong re-rating) and N3 (operational deterioration). A production run-rate consistent with 200,000+ oz per annum at AISC below US$1,200/oz confirms the earnings thesis; a miss signals either operational or security disruption.
rows.currentReading: Not yet available -- Q1 2026 quarterly report expected April 2026

rows.evidence: Kiaka Gold Project Final Investment Decision (FID) and financing structure -- the most powerful single catalyst for distinguishing between the N4 transformational scenario and the N2 steady-state base case. An FID with secured project financing would confirm management's ability to execute growth and would force a material upward revision to NAV estimates.
rows.currentReading: No FID announced as of March 17, 2026; development status unclear from available evidence

rows.evidence: Gold price trajectory over the next 3-6 months -- at US$5,024/oz, gold is at a generational high and the sustainability of this level is the most important macro discriminator. A sustained gold price above US$4,500/oz validates N1 earnings power; a reversal below US$3,500/oz would shift weight decisively toward N3 and compress the earnings multiple.
rows.currentReading: US$5,024/oz (+2.9% on March 17, 2026) -- at generational highs

discriminators.nonDiscriminating:
• ASX Governance Compliance: The filing of an Appendix 4G and Corporate Governance Statement is a standard ASX requirement and does not differentiate between the bull, base, or bear scenarios -- all listed companies must comply regardless of operational performance.
  • 52-Week Price Range (A$2.08 - A$3.91): The historical price range describes where the stock has been, not where it is going. It is equally consistent with N1 (stock recovering toward A$3.91) and N3 (stock retesting A$2.08).
  • Securities Cessation Notice: The expiry of unlisted options or performance rights is a routine administrative event that does not provide meaningful signal about operational performance or future earnings trajectory.
  • Market Cap (Not Available): The absence of a reported market cap figure is a data gap, not an analytical signal, and does not discriminate between any of the four hypotheses.

tripwires.intro: The following catalysts could force a material revision to hypothesis weightings. Each carries conditional outcomes that map to specific narrative shifts.

conditions.if: If Sanbrado Q1 production exceeds 50,000oz with AISC below US$1,200/oz
conditions.then: Then operational momentum is confirmed, supporting N1 re-rating thesis and suggesting FY2026 NPAT could match or exceed FY2025 at current gold prices

conditions.if: If Q1 production misses materially (below 40,000oz) or AISC spikes above US$1,500/oz
conditions.then: Then N3 jurisdictional/operational risk scenario gains weight, likely pushing the stock toward the A$2.08 52-week low

cards.name: Kiaka Development Decision

conditions.if: If WAF announces a positive Final Investment Decision (FID) for Kiaka with financing secured
conditions.then: Then N4 tail scenario activates, potentially driving the stock toward A$5.00+ as the market prices in a multi-asset production profile and increased M&A attractiveness

conditions.if: If Kiaka FID is deferred beyond 2026 or capital cost estimates increase materially
conditions.then: Then growth optionality is discounted further, reinforcing the single-asset discount and capping upside at the N2 base case range of A$3.00-3.50

cards.source: WAF Investor Presentation March 2026; Kiaka project development timeline

cards.name: Burkina Faso Security and Political Stability

conditions.if: If Burkina Faso security conditions stabilise and the transitional government extends or reaffirms WAF's mining agreements
conditions.then: Then the jurisdictional risk premium compresses, supporting a re-rating toward N1 bull case and potentially closing the gap to the 52-week high of A$3.91

conditions.if: If security incidents escalate near Sanbrado or the government moves to renegotiate mining terms
conditions.then: Then N3 bear case dominates, with the stock potentially breaking below the 52-week low of A$2.08 as operational continuity is questioned

cards.source: Burkina Faso political risk monitoring; WAF operational updates

gaps.analyticalLimitations: This initiation is materially constrained by the absence of detailed financial statements, operational metrics, and broker research. The A$567M NPAT headline is confirmed but without cash flow statements, balance sheet data, or cost breakdowns, it is impossible to assess the quality of earnings, leverage, or sustainability of the profit. The lack of AISC data is a critical gap for a gold producer, as cost structure is the primary determinant of earnings sensitivity to gold price movements. Jurisdictional risk assessment is qualitative only, as no independent security assessments or government relations updates were available. All valuation ranges in this report are indicative and should be treated as directional only, pending a full financial model build using the 2025 Annual Report data.

trend.structure: Price in downtrend relative to 50-day and 200-day moving averages.

footer.disclaimer: This report does not constitute personal financial advice. Continuum Intelligence synthesises cross-domain evidence using the Analysis of Competing Hypotheses (ACH) methodology. All factual claims are sourced from ASX filings, company disclosures, broker consensus data, and publicly available information.

hero.embedded_thesis: At A$2.94, the market appears to be pricing WAF as a high-risk, single-jurisdiction producer with a meaningful probability of operational disruption, despite the company having just delivered A$567M NPAT for FY2025. The current price implies the market is applying a substantial discount -- likely 30-40% relative to Australian-domiciled peers on equivalent earnings -- to reflect Burkina Faso political and security risk. With gold at US$5,024/oz, the implied earnings yield at the current price is exceptionally high, suggesting either the market believes current gold prices are unsustainable, or that jurisdictional risk is severe enough to warrant a persistent discount. The 25% gap between the current price and the 52-week high of A$3.91 suggests the market has already partially de-rated the stock from its recent peak, possibly on Burkina Faso risk concerns or profit-taking after the strong FY2025 result.
hero.skew_description: The hypothesis weightings assign 40% to the base case (steady-state producer with jurisdictional discount), 35% to the bull case (gold-driven re-rating), 18% to the bear case (jurisdictional deterioration), and 7% to the tail catalyst (Kiaka-driven transformation or M&A). The combined upside scenarios (42%) modestly outweigh the downside (18%), producing a net bullish skew, though the large base case weight reflects genuine uncertainty about the pace and magnitude of any re-rating.

position_in_range.note: World prices are indicative -- pending full W1-W4 valuation build. Bear case reflects Burkina Faso operational disruption. Base case reflects steady-state Sanbrado production at US$4,200/oz gold. Bull case reflects sustained US$5,000/oz gold with Kiaka optionality. Catalyst case reflects Kiaka construction approval plus M&A premium.

next_decision_point.event: Q1 2026 Quarterly Activities Report (Production and Cost Update)
next_decision_point.metric: Sanbrado gold production (oz), All-In Sustaining Cost (AISC, US$/oz), and any Kiaka development update
next_decision_point.thresholds: Production above 50,000oz for Q1 and AISC below US$1,200/oz would confirm operational momentum and support N1. Any production miss or AISC blowout above US$1,500/oz would shift weight toward N3. A Kiaka construction decision announcement would be an immediate N4 trigger.

goldAgent.executive_summary: West African Resources is a multi-asset gold producer operating entirely in Burkina Faso, with FY2025 group production of 300,383 oz at AISC of US$1,488/oz, meeting guidance for the fifth consecutive year. The primary growth asset, Kiaka SA (85% owned, in ramp-up), is subject to an unresolved Burkina Faso government demand for an additional 35% for paid consideration, which if granted would reduce WAF's economic interest to approximately 50% and materially impair NAV. The group ended CY2025 with net cash of approximately US$210m (A$323m), gross cash of A$584m and A$177m unsold bullion, reflecting strong free cash flow generation at elevated gold prices. All three subsidiaries have had state equity increased from 10% to 15% for nil proceeds, with non-discretionary priority dividends applying to subsidiary profits under OHADA accounting, creating an ongoing and escalating fiscal drag.

investment_view.bull_case: Kiaka SA ownership dispute resolved at minimal dilution to WAF, full grid power drives Kiaka AISC structurally below US$1,400/oz, group production ramps toward the 480,000 oz/year 10-year target, 6.5 Moz reserve base supports long mine life, and gold price remains elevated above US$3,500/oz generating substantial free cash flow and balance sheet optionality including buybacks, dividends, or Toega development.
investment_view.base_case: Kiaka SA ownership is resolved with WAF retaining approximately 70-85% economic interest at some consideration outflow, Kiaka AISC continues declining through 2026 toward US$1,500/oz range, group production steps up to 380,000-430,000 oz in 2026, net cash position grows but is partially offset by priority dividends and elevated royalties at high gold prices, and Toega development timeline remains uncertain pending Kiaka stabilisation.
investment_view.bear_case: Burkina Faso government obtains additional 35% of Kiaka SA, reducing WAF's effective interest to approximately 50% of its primary growth asset; further nil-proceeds equity transfers or royalty rate increases are imposed; Kiaka ramp-up underperforms on grade or throughput; and sliding-scale royalties reaching 10-12% at spot gold prices structurally compress margins at the subsidiary level.
what_must_be_true: Kiaka SA ownership dispute must be resolved without WAF's economic interest falling below approximately 70% to preserve NAV materiality of the growth asset
Kiaka site AISC must decline sustainably as grid power replaces diesel and ore blend improves toward the 248,000 oz/year steady-state target
Burkina Faso sovereign and fiscal risk must not escalate further beyond the current royalty structure and 15% state equity
Group production must step up materially from the 300,383 oz FY2025 base as Kiaka ramps to justify the medium-term 480,000 oz/year target
Balance sheet net cash must be preserved or grown to provide a buffer against sovereign risk and fund Toega development
investment_view.monitoring_trigger: Any formal announcement regarding the outcome of the Burkina Faso government's demand for an additional 35% of Kiaka SA, or any further nil-proceeds equity transfer, royalty rate change, or suspension of WAF from ASX trading

valuation.valuation_notes: No NAV, NPV, IRR, P/NAV, or EV multiples are disclosed or derivable from corpus. Market cap and enterprise value are not available in the corpus. No share price or shares on issue data provided. The primary valuation risk is the unresolved Kiaka SA sovereignty dispute: if WAF's interest falls from 85% to 50%, the NAV of the primary growth asset would be approximately halved at the subsidiary level, with a materially negative impact on group NAV. The 6.5 Moz reserve and 12.2 Moz resource base are large for an ASX-listed producer, but no grade or tonne breakdown is available to underpin a reserve-based valuation. Net cash of approximately US$210m (A$323m) at 31 December 2025 provides a floor to valuation and reduces near-term dilution risk. Sliding-scale royalties at 10% at Q4 2025 realised gold price of US$4,058/oz represent a meaningful drag on NAV versus simple AISC-based models.

peer_frame.relative_valuation_comment: No peer valuation data available in corpus. WAF is a meaningful West African gold producer by reserve size (6.5 Moz) and production scale, operating entirely in Burkina Faso. Key peers include Perseus Mining (multi-jurisdiction West Africa, lower sovereign risk), Endeavour Mining (diversified West Africa, larger scale), and Resolute Mining (Mali exposure, comparable sovereign risk). WAF's sliding-scale royalty structure and unresolved Kiaka ownership dispute likely warrant a meaningful sovereign risk discount relative to peers with more diversified jurisdictional exposure.

goldAgent.management_assessment: Management has demonstrated consistent operational delivery, meeting FY2025 production and cost guidance for the fifth consecutive year. This track record is a positive indicator of mine management capability. However, the corpus does not provide sufficient information to assess management's capital allocation history, M&A track record, dilution history, mine build experience, or incentive alignment. The handling of the Kiaka ownership dispute and ASX suspension is a material governance and strategic management event whose outcome is unresolved; the outcome of this negotiation will be a primary determinant of management quality assessment. The company's rapid transition from net debt of A$132.8m at June 2025 to net cash of approximately US$210m by December 2025 demonstrates strong cash generation capability and financial discipline at current gold prices.

recommendation.reason: The unresolved Burkina Faso sovereign demand for an additional 35% of Kiaka SA is a binary, high-impact risk that cannot be adequately priced without knowing the outcome or compensation terms. If granted at full scope, WAF's economic interest in its primary growth asset falls to approximately 50%, materially impairing NAV. This risk, combined with the pattern of escalating sovereign and fiscal demands (nil-proceeds equity transfer to 15%, royalty rate increase, OHADA priority dividends), the concentration of all assets in a single high-risk jurisdiction, and the absence of reserve grade, metallurgical, capex, and NAV data in the corpus, makes a well-founded investment recommendation impossible at this time. Deferral is recommended pending resolution of the Kiaka ownership dispute and release of 2026 guidance.

evidence.label: FY2025 production and AISC guidance met
evidence.finding: Group produced 300,383 oz against guidance of 290,000-360,000 oz; group AISC US$1,488/oz (A$2,308/oz at 0.6448 average FX); fifth consecutive year of guidance achievement

evidence.label: Q4 2025 operating cash flow and realised price
evidence.finding: Q4 2025 operating cash flow A$389m after A$48m income tax; average realised price US$4,058/oz; group AISC US$1,561/oz; A$28m principal repaid on borrowings

evidence.label: Net cash position at 31 December 2025
evidence.finding: Net cash approximately US$210m (A$323m at 0.65 AUD/USD); gross cash A$584m; unsold gold bullion A$177m

evidence.label: Kiaka SA sovereign demand - unresolved
evidence.finding: Burkina Faso government formally requested additional 35% of Kiaka SA for paid consideration in August 2025; discussions active as at December 2025 quarterly; if granted, WAF economic interest falls from 85% to approximately 50%
evidence.source: WAF December 2025 Quarterly; WAF September 2025 Quarterly

evidence.label: ASX trading suspension
evidence.finding: WAF suspended from ASX from 1 September 2025, reinstated 25 November 2025, in connection with Kiaka SA sovereign demand disclosure
evidence.source: WAF December 2025 Quarterly; WAF September 2025 Quarterly

evidence.label: State equity increase to 15% for nil proceeds
evidence.finding: State equity in SOMISA, Kiaka SA and Toega SA increased from 10% to 15% for nil proceeds in July-August 2025; WAF's interest reduced from 90% to 85%; A$33.4m reduction in retained profits recorded in H1 2025

evidence.label: OHADA priority dividends
evidence.finding: 15% state equity entitles State to non-discretionary priority dividend equal to 15% of subsidiary profits under OHADA accounting; SOMISA paid A$99m to State in Q3 2025; WAF provisioned A$26.4m for SOMISA H1 2025 priority dividend
evidence.source: WAF H1 2025 Financial Report; WAF September 2025 Quarterly

evidence.label: Sliding-scale royalty structure
evidence.finding: Gross production royalty 7% at US$2,000-3,000/oz, rising 1% per US$500/oz above US$3,000/oz to 12% above US$5,500/oz; effective April 2025 base rate increased by 1%; 1% community development levy at all levels; 10% applicable at Q4 2025 realised price of US$4,058/oz
evidence.source: WAF December 2025 Quarterly; WAF H1 2025 Financial Report

evidence.label: Ore Reserves and Mineral Resources August 2025
evidence.finding: Ore Reserves 6.5 Moz gold; Mineral Resources 12.2 Moz gold (JORC, August 2025 update); grade, tonnes and classification breakdown not available in corpus
evidence.source: WAF H1 2025 Financial Report; WAF September 2025 Quarterly

evidence.label: 10-year production target
evidence.finding: 10-year production target averaging 480,000 oz/year from 2025 to 2034, peak 569,000 oz in 2029; Kiaka target 248,000 oz/year from 2026; Sanbrado mine plan extended to 2035
evidence.source: WAF H1 2025 Financial Report; WAF September 2025 Quarterly

evidence.label: Kiaka grid power connection and cost trajectory
evidence.finding: Grid power contributed to Kiaka site in December 2025; expected to enable higher throughput and treatment of harder, higher-grade ore; Kiaka site sustaining cost fell from US$1,921/oz (Q3 2025, partial quarter) to US$1,649/oz (Q4 2025, first full quarter)
evidence.source: WAF December 2025 Quarterly; WAF September 2025 Quarterly

evidence.label: Net debt at 30 June 2025 and transition to net cash
evidence.finding: Net debt A$132.8m at 30 June 2025; transitioning to notional net cash US$33m at 30 September 2025 and US$210m at 31 December 2025; secured facilities US$265m combined limit from Sprott Resource Lending Corp and Coris Bank International, fully drawn at 30 June 2025
evidence.source: WAF H1 2025 Financial Report; WAF September 2025 Quarterly; WAF December 2025 Quarterly

evidence.label: 2026 guidance not yet released
evidence.finding: Annual guidance for 2026 planned for Q1 2026 release; not available in corpus as at analysis date

goldAgent.verdict: West African Resources is a multi-asset gold producer operating entirely in Burkina Faso, with FY2025 group production of 300,383 oz at AISC of US$1,488/oz, meeting guidance for the fifth consecutive year. The primary growth asset, Kiaka SA (85% owned, in ramp-up), is subject to an unresolved Burkina Faso government demand for an additional 35% for paid consideration, which if granted would reduce WAF's economic interest to approximately 50% and materially impair NAV. The group ended CY2025 with net cash of approximately US$210m (A$323m), gross cash of A$584m and A$177m unsold bullion, reflecting strong free cash flow generation at elevated gold prices. All three subsidiaries have had state equity increased from 10% to 15% for nil proceeds, with non-discretionary priority dividends applying to subsidiary profits under OHADA accounting, creating an ongoing and escalating fiscal drag.

hypothesis.bull: Kiaka SA ownership dispute resolved at minimal dilution to WAF, full grid power drives Kiaka AISC structurally below US$1,400/oz, group production ramps toward the 480,000 oz/year 10-year target, 6.5 Moz reserve base supports long mine life, and gold price remains elevated above US$3,500/oz generating substantial free cash flow and balance sheet optionality including buybacks, dividends, or Toega development.
hypothesis.bear: Burkina Faso government obtains additional 35% of Kiaka SA, reducing WAF's effective interest to approximately 50% of its primary growth asset; further nil-proceeds equity transfers or royalty rate increases are imposed; Kiaka ramp-up underperforms on grade or throughput; and sliding-scale royalties reaching 10-12% at spot gold prices structurally compress margins at the subsidiary level.

goldAgent.monitoring_trigger: Any formal announcement regarding the outcome of the Burkina Faso government's demand for an additional 35% of Kiaka SA, or any further nil-proceeds equity transfer, royalty rate change, or suspension of WAF from ASX trading

goldAnalysis.executive_summary: West African Resources is a multi-asset gold producer operating entirely in Burkina Faso, with FY2025 production of 300,383 oz and a 10-year target averaging 480,000 oz/year from 2025–2034 underpinned by 6.5 Moz in ore reserves and 12.2 Moz in mineral resources. The company has rapidly transitioned to a net cash position of approximately US$210m by end-2025 on the back of elevated gold prices, with AISC of US$1,488/oz for FY2025. The dominant near-term risk is an unresolved Burkina Faso government demand for an additional 35% of the primary growth asset Kiaka SA, which if granted would reduce WAF's economic interest from 85% to approximately 50%; this forced an ASX trading suspension from September to November 2025. The fiscal burden has also increased via a sliding-scale royalty reaching 10% at prevailing gold prices and a nil-proceeds state equity increase to 15% in all three operating subsidiaries.

investment_view.bull_case: The Kiaka ramp-up delivers 248,000 oz/year from 2026 at declining AISC as full grid power replaces diesel and ore blend improves; the government's 35% equity demand is resolved with no additional dilution or at modest paid consideration; the group reaches 480,000+ oz/year average production by 2027, generating substantial free cash flow at spot gold prices well above US$3,000/oz, and the strong balance sheet (US$210m net cash) is deployed in shareholder returns or accretive exploration.
investment_view.base_case: Kiaka ramp-up continues through 2026, approaching target production of 248,000 oz/year; group production approaches 450,000–480,000 oz/year by 2027; the sovereignty dispute is resolved with WAF retaining 70–85% of Kiaka SA at acceptable paid consideration; AISC stabilises below US$1,500/oz as grid power becomes fully operational; the balance sheet provides a buffer against further fiscal impositions. Mine life extends to 2035 at Sanbrado.
investment_view.bear_case: The Burkina Faso government successfully claims an additional 35% of Kiaka SA for minimal consideration, reducing WAF's interest to ~50%; further sovereign fiscal impositions (royalty increases, additional equity demands across SOMISA or Toega) erode economics; Kiaka ramp-up stalls due to infrastructure, ore blend, or security issues; gold price retreats toward US$2,500/oz compressing margins against a high fixed royalty burden; and security deterioration in Burkina Faso disrupts operations.
what_must_be_true: The Burkina Faso government's demand for an additional 35% of Kiaka SA must be resolved without full dilution of WAF's interest to 50% or lower.
Kiaka must successfully complete its ramp-up to approximately 248,000 oz/year production in 2026 as grid power becomes fully operational.
Group AISC must trend below US$1,500/oz as Kiaka matures and the ore blend improves, to maintain adequate margins at any gold price softening.
No further nil-proceeds equity transfers or materially adverse royalty increases must be imposed by the Burkina Faso government across SOMISA, Kiaka SA or Toega SA.
Security conditions in Burkina Faso must remain sufficient to allow uninterrupted mine access and supply chain operations.
The US$265m secured debt facility must be refinanced or repaid without triggering adverse covenant breaches as gold price and production assumptions evolve.
investment_view.monitoring_trigger: Any formal resolution or escalation of the Burkina Faso government's demand for an additional 35% of Kiaka SA, including binding arbitration, negotiated agreement, or government decree; and release of Q1 2026 production/cost guidance confirming Kiaka ramp-up trajectory.

valuation.valuation_notes: No NAV, NPV, IRR, or market capitalisation data is available in the corpus. No peer multiples or comparable transaction data were provided. EV/oz metrics cannot be computed without market cap or debt maturity data. The net cash position of US$210m at 31 December 2025 (plus A$177m in unsold bullion) provides a partial balance sheet anchor. Valuation is dominated by resolution of the Kiaka sovereign equity demand and the Kiaka ramp-up trajectory; until those are resolved, any NAV calculation carries high uncertainty. Gross cash of A$584m at year-end is a material positive offset against the US$265m debt facility.

peer_frame.relative_valuation_comment: No peer valuation data is available in the corpus. WAF operates in Burkina Faso, a jurisdiction that attracts a material sovereign risk discount relative to West African peers operating in Ghana, Côte d'Ivoire, or Senegal. At 6.5 Moz reserves and a 10-year production target averaging 480,000 oz/year, the asset scale is comparable to mid-tier producers, but the unresolved equity demand on Kiaka SA and the elevated sliding-scale royalty (10% at current gold prices) are meaningful valuation headwinds versus peers with more stable fiscal regimes. Peer comparison on EV/reserve oz and P/NAV cannot be completed without current market data.

top_failure_modes: Burkina Faso government successfully imposes additional 35% equity transfer in Kiaka SA, reducing WAF's economic interest to approximately 50% and materially impairing the primary growth asset's value.
Pattern of sovereign equity demands extends to SOMISA (Sanbrado) or Toega SA, further eroding WAF's effective economic interest across its entire portfolio.
Kiaka ramp-up stalls or is materially delayed due to infrastructure constraints, security disruptions, or ore blend issues, preventing the step-change to 480,000+ oz/year group production.
Escalation of security conditions in Burkina Faso disrupting mine access, supply chains, or workforce safety.
Gold price correction toward US$2,500/oz compresses margins against a structurally high sliding-scale royalty and elevated AISC during ramp-up.
Further adverse fiscal measures (additional royalty increases, windfall taxes, export restrictions on gold) imposed by the Burkina Faso government.
technical_red_flags: Reserve grade in g/t not publicly disclosed in corpus – inability to independently assess reserve quality or dilution risk.
Metallurgical recovery rates not disclosed for any asset – cannot assess processing risk or upside.
Kiaka AISC of US$1,921/oz in Q3 2025 (first partial quarter) is very high; Q4 2025 improvement to US$1,649/oz still materially above group AISC, indicating ramp-up risk remains.

Grid power dependency at Kiaka – full transition from diesel backup not yet complete as of December 2025 quarterly; cost improvement contingent on infrastructure delivery outside WAF's direct control.
No capex data (initial or sustaining) disclosed for Kiaka or Toega – capital intensity and return metrics cannot be independently assessed.
Ore blend at Kiaka described as sub-optimal during ramp-up; harder ore blend dependency on full grid power introduces throughput uncertainty.
Deposit type, mining method, and geotechnical parameters not disclosed for any asset.
Reconciliation risk flagged in corpus
Metallurgical complexity: refractory ore
Operational risk: dilution pressure referenced
Operational risk: ore loss referenced
Accounting risk: capitalised stripping treatment mentioned
hard_risk_flags: Unresolved Burkina Faso government demand for additional 35% of Kiaka SA – formal request received August 2025; led to ASX trading suspension 1 September to 25 November 2025; discussions active as of December 2025 quarterly.
Nil-proceeds state equity increase from 10% to 15% in SOMISA, Kiaka SA, and Toega SA completed July–August 2025 – represents a precedent for uncompensated dilution.

SOMISA paid A$99m in non-discretionary priority dividends to the State in Q3 2025 under OHADA accounting principles; additional A$26.4m provisioned for H1 2025 – structural cash leakage that is non-discretionary and recurring.

Sliding-scale royalty reaching 10% at current gold prices (US$4,058/oz average Q4 2025) is a material gross revenue deduction that accelerates at higher gold prices, creating a natural cap on margin expansion.
Burkina Faso is rated as a high-risk security and political jurisdiction; operations entirely concentrated in this single country with no geographic diversification.

goldAnalysis.management_assessment: WAF's management has demonstrated a credible operational track record, having met production and cost guidance for five consecutive years through FY2025. This is a material positive for a West African operator in a challenging jurisdiction. However, the corpus provides limited insight into board composition, mine-build experience beyond Sanbrado, capital allocation discipline, dilution history, M&A record, or incentive alignment. The response to the Kiaka SA sovereign equity demand – including managing the ASX suspension and reinstatement – will be the defining test of management's ability to navigate sovereign risk. The speed of balance sheet repair (from net debt of A$132.8m at June 2025 to net cash of US$210m by December 2025) reflects strong treasury management in a high gold price environment. Insufficient information is available to assess management depth, succession planning, or ESG governance comprehensively.

recommendation.reason: WAF operates a growing, reserve-rich gold business in Burkina Faso with a strong operational track record and rapidly improving balance sheet. However, the unresolved government demand for an additional 35% of Kiaka SA – the primary growth asset – represents a binary, existential risk to the investment thesis that cannot be sized or priced with available information. Until this sovereignty dispute is formally resolved, the risk-adjusted return profile is too uncertain for a Proceed recommendation. Additionally, the complete absence of reserve grade, metallurgical recovery, capex, and deposit type data in the corpus prevents independent technical underwriting. A Defer position is appropriate pending: (1) formal resolution of the Kiaka SA equity demand; (2) release of 2026 production/cost guidance confirming Kiaka ramp-up; and (3) access to a current technical report providing reserve grade, metallurgy, and capex data. If the equity demand is resolved favourably and Kiaka ramp-up confirms the production trajectory, the asset base at 6.5 Moz reserves with a 10-year production target of 480,000 oz/year and a strong balance sheet would support a reassessment toward Proceed.

evidence.label: Group FY2025 production
evidence.finding: WAF produced 300,383 oz in FY2025, meeting guidance of 290,000–360,000 oz for the fifth consecutive year.

evidence.label: Group AISC FY2025
evidence.finding: Group All-in Sustaining Cost for FY2025 was US$1,488/oz (A$2,308/oz at average FX of 0.6448).

evidence.label: Group AISC Q4 2025
evidence.finding: Group AISC for Q4 2025 was US$1,561/oz, driven by the sliding-scale royalty at an average realised gold price of US$4,058/oz.

evidence.label: Ore Reserves and Mineral Resources
evidence.finding: As of August 2025, WAF's Ore Reserves are 6.5 Moz gold and Mineral Resources are 12.2 Moz gold. No breakdown by category or reserve grade in g/t provided.
evidence.source: WAF August 2025 resource/reserve update (referenced in corpus)

evidence.label: 10-year production target
evidence.finding: WAF has a 10-year production target averaging 480,000 oz/year from 2025 to 2034, peaking at 569,000 oz in 2029. Sanbrado mine plan extended to 2035.
evidence.source: WAF corporate disclosures / December 2025 Quarterly Activities Report

evidence.label: Kiaka production target and ramp-up
evidence.finding: Kiaka has a target of 248,000 oz/year from 2026. Grid power contributed in December 2025, enabling higher throughput and harder ore processing. Sustaining costs fell from US$1,921/oz in Q3 2025 to US$1,649/oz in Q4 2025.

evidence.label: Balance sheet – net cash position
evidence.finding: WAF transitioned from net debt of A$132.8m at 30 June 2025 to net cash of US$210m (~A$323m) by 31 December 2025. Gross cash at year-end was A$584m, including A$177m in unsold gold bullion. Borrowings comprised US$265m secured facilities (Sprott and Coris Bank), fully drawn at 30 June 2025.

evidence.label: Burkina Faso state equity increase
evidence.finding: State equity in SOMISA, Kiaka SA, and Toega SA increased from 10% to 15% in July–August 2025 for nil proceeds, reducing WAF's economic interest to 85% across all three subsidiaries.

evidence.label: Sovereign demand – additional 35% Kiaka SA
evidence.finding: Burkina Faso government formally requested an additional 35% of Kiaka SA for paid consideration in August 2025. This led to WAF's ASX suspension from 1 September to 25 November 2025. Discussions remained active as of December 2025 quarterly. If granted, WAF's interest in Kiaka SA falls from 85% to approximately 50%.

evidence.label: Royalty structure – Burkina Faso
evidence.finding: Gross production royalty: 7% for gold US$2,000–3,000/oz, rising by 1% per US$500/oz above US$3,000/oz (12% above US$5,500/oz), plus 1% community development levy at all price levels. Base rate increased by 1% effective April 2025. At Q4 2025 average price of US$4,058/oz, applicable royalty rate was 10%.

evidence.label: State priority dividend – OHADA
evidence.finding: State's 15% equity entitles it to an annual non-discretionary priority dividend equal to 15% of subsidiary profits under OHADA accounting. SOMISA paid A$99m to State in Q3 2025 (15% of retained earnings to December 2024). WAF provisioned A$26.4m for SOMISA H1 2025 priority dividend.

evidence.label: Accounting – state equity transfer impact
evidence.finding: A$33.4m reduction in retained profits recorded in WAF's H1 2025 consolidated statement due to the nil-proceeds state equity transfer.
evidence.source: WAF H1 2025 financial statements (referenced in corpus)

evidence.label: Q4 2025 operating cash flow
evidence.finding: Q4 2025 operating cash flow was A$389m after A$48m in income tax.
